question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getPaginationLink() is called on every response to GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts, and by crafting requests that always return exactly limit rows (triggering next link generation), sustain a chain of expensive 3-way UNION ALL queries indefinitely without repeating any request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination logic in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() where response.links.next is set when rewards.length === query.limit, by targeting an account with exactly limit staking rewards per time window and following next links, sustaining a continuous stream of two-DB-query requests without repeating any request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a very wide token.id range (token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:9999999999999) and no serial_number filter, causing getLowerFilters() to produce a single subquery with both lower and upper bounds on token_id but no secondary constraint, scanning the entire token_id range for the given account_id at maximum limit?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum number of spender.id=eq:X values alongside a 3-way UNION ALL scenario, causing NftService.getSubQuery() to be called three times each time pushing all spenderIdInFilters values into the shared params array, resulting in a params array that grows with each subquery call and a query with many bound parameters, increasing query parse and plan time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a token.id=eq:X and serial_number=eq:Y (both equal filters), causing getLowerFilters() to include all four filters (primary.equal, primary.lower, primary.upper, secondary.equal) in the lower array, and then follow the pagination link which sets inclusive=false for the primary bound, generating a new query with token_id > X, scanning the next token range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with timestamp=lte:9999999999999999999 (maximum nanosecond timestamp) and maximum limit, causing the staking_reward_transfer query to scan from the beginning of time up to the maximum timestamp, maximizing the range of rows evaluated?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with no filters and maximum limit against an account with a large NFT portfolio, causing NftService.getNfts() to fetch maximum rows from the nft table with a left join on entity, then map each row to a NftViewModel object in JavaScript, consuming both DB I/O and application-side CPU for object construction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=gte:X alongside the maximum number of timestamp=eq:Z values to GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards, causing extractStakingRewardsQuery() to build both a range condition (srt.consensus_timestamp >= $3) and a large IN clause (srt.consensus_timestamp in ($4,...,$N)), forcing the DB to evaluate both predicates and potentially preventing optimal index usage?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999 (wide spender range) combined with a 3-way UNION ALL scenario, causing each of the three subqueries to evaluate the spender range condition against every row matching account_id, multiplying the spender filter evaluation cost by three?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with a very tight timestamp range (timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:X+1) that matches many rows, causing the DB to evaluate the range predicate and return up to limit rows, then the application to map each to StakingRewardTransferViewModel, consuming CPU proportional to the result set size?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a token.id=gte:X&serial_number=lte:Y combination (lower bound on primary, upper bound on secondary), causing getUpperFilters() to return empty (no primary upper) and getLowerFilters() to include both bounds in the lower array, resulting in a single subquery but with a cross-column filter that may not use a composite index efficiently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:999999999&serial_number=gte:1&serial_number=lte:999999999 and the maximum number of spender.id=eq:X values, creating the worst-case scenario of a 3-way UNION ALL where each subquery has a large IN clause on spender, maximizing both query complexity and DB CPU usage in a single request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a valid alias path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getAccountIdFromAlias() which executes entityFromAliasQuery (a DB query with a WHERE alias = $1 condition), adding an extra DB round-trip before the NFT query, increasing per-request DB load compared to numeric ID requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with an EVM address path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() (DB query), then isValidAccount() to call entityExistenceQuery (another DB query), resulting in three sequential DB queries per request (EVM lookup + existence check + rewards query)?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with order=asc and maximum limit, causing the DB to sort the nft result set ascending by (token_id, serial_number), and then immediately follow the next link which also uses order=asc, sustaining a sequence of maximum-limit ascending sort queries that keep the DB sort buffer occupied?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=lte:X alongside the maximum number of timestamp=eq:Z values to GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards, causing extractStakingRewardsQuery() to build a query with both an upper range condition and a large IN clause, where the IN clause values may fall outside the range, forcing the DB to evaluate both predicates redundantly?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a token.id=eq:X filter and the maximum number of spender.id=eq:Z values, causing NftService.getSubQuery() to build a query with account_id = $1 AND token_id = $3 AND spender IN ($4,...,$N), where the large IN clause is evaluated against all NFTs of that token for the account, increasing per-row evaluation cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with maximum limit and no timestamp filter against an account with a large staking history, causing the application to call stakingRewardsTransfers.map() to construct limit StakingRewardTransferViewModel objects in JavaScript, consuming application-side memory and CPU proportional to the limit value?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with maximum limit and no filters, causing nonFungibleTokens.map((nft) => new NftViewModel(nft)) to construct up to limit NftViewModel objects in JavaScript, consuming application-side memory and CPU, and then getPaginationLink() to compute the next link by accessing lastRow[primaryBound.viewModelKey] and lastRow[secondaryBound.viewModelKey]?
